chains.name: Tech

signals.note: Slightly above average, within normal range

signals.source: USPTO patent filings by tracked companies (24h)

chains.conclusion: Only one RISING signal in the Tech chain today: Crunchbase tech funding news items came in at 2 versus a 30-day average of 1.23, a 62% spike suggesting a small cluster of funding announcements in AI or autonomous driving. Hacker News engagement is DROPPING sharply — 12,245 versus a 30-day average of 15,789, a 22% decline — indicating reduced community discussion around monitored keywords such as protein folding, quantum computing, and autonomous driving. arXiv submissions are flat. Overall convergence is 1, which is noise-level.

chains.name: Economy

signals.source: Sector layoff news items (24h Google News count)

chains.conclusion: The Economy chain shows one critical DROPPING signal: Adzuna sector job posting volume collapsed to 1,236 today versus a 30-day average of 36,484 — a 97% drop. This is almost certainly a data feed anomaly or API disruption rather than a genuine labor market event of this magnitude, but it must be flagged. The yield spread is flat at 0.52, consistent with a mildly positive curve. Layoff news is slightly below average at 44 items versus 48. The cardboard box production index is SILENT. Convergence is 1; no actionable signal.

chains.name: Biotech

signals.source: NIH Reporter active grants this FY

chains.conclusion: NIH Reporter active grants reached 13,181 this fiscal year versus a 30-day average of 11,210 — a 17.6% increase that falls just short of the 20% RISING threshold but signals meaningful acceleration in federal funding deployment, likely into priority areas such as GLP-1 obesity research, oncology ADC programmes, and CRISPR gene editing given the monitored keyword set. bioRxiv preprints are DROPPING sharply to zero versus an average of 201, which may reflect a weekend or platform disruption. S-1 biotech filings are also zero. Convergence is 1; noise level.

chains.name: Social

signals.source: Google Trends avg interest score (7d window)
Amazon Movers and Shakers item count

chains.conclusion: Google Trends average interest score for monitored terms — autonomous vehicle, robotaxi, protein folding, small modular reactor — rose to 34.25 versus a 30-day average of 24.375, a 40% increase that qualifies as RISING. This suggests a meaningful uptick in public search interest around autonomous vehicles and possibly robotaxi deployments or SMR energy news. Bluesky is SILENT today. Kickstarter and Amazon signals are flat. Convergence is 1; insufficient for a formal signal, but the Google Trends spike is worth monitoring for a second consecutive day.

chains.name: Geopolitics

signals.source: % of tracked commodities above own 30d baseline

chains.conclusion: The Geopolitics chain shows one DROPPING signal: the percentage of tracked commodities — lithium, copper, wheat, crude oil, gold — trading above their 30-day baselines fell to 80 today versus a 30-day average of 650.19. This dramatic drop indicates that commodity prices across the basket have broadly retreated below their recent baselines, suggesting either a broad risk-off move in raw materials or a data normalisation effect. Shipping congestion, EU consultations, and Congressional hearings are all flat at zero. Convergence is 0; noise.

chains.name: Corporate

signals.source: SEC Form 4 insider filings for watchlist (24h)
Keyword-matched job postings across watchlist companies
USPTO patent filings by watchlist companies (24h)

chains.conclusion: SEC Form 4 insider filings for the watchlist surged to 6 today versus a 30-day average of 1.97 — a 205% spike, the strongest signal in the Corporate chain. This volume of insider transactions across NVIDIA, Google, Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly, Moderna, WeRide, Tempus AI, Recursion Pharmaceuticals, Zscaler, and peers warrants close attention. However, per the per-company data, no insider purchases were recorded — the Form 4 activity likely reflects sales or other transactions, which do not constitute bullish Signal B evidence. Job postings, patents, and 8-K filings are flat. Convergence is 1.

chains.name: Energy

signals.source: Average energy commodity price (lithium, uranium, copper)

chains.conclusion: The Energy chain shows one RISING signal: the average price of the lithium, uranium, and copper basket reached 3,199.84 versus a 30-day average of 3,072.01 — a 4.2% increase that does not meet the 20% threshold for RISING but is directionally positive. Note: the scoring rules require >20% above average for RISING; this source is FLAT. ARPA-E is SILENT. arXiv applied physics preprints are flat. Crunchbase energy funding is zero. Convergence is 0 after correct scoring; no actionable signal.
